name: AXS Retest Hold De-Risk v1
thesis: AXS 4h is the structurally different lane for testing retest-and-hold continuation that de-risks by time if expansion fails to appear fast enough. The edge should come from delayed confirmation after a retest, not from immediate breakout chasing.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest holds are too rare, if the retest qualifier conflicts with direction confirmation, or if continuation is too weak to justify even the faster de-risking profile.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout does not extend immediately, retests the hold zone, then continues only if acceptance is real. If the second leg does not appear quickly, exposure should be reduced early.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Markets in or near chop often punish immediate breakout entries. A delayed retest entry with quick time-based de-risk can preserve expectancy if continuation exists only intermittently.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is useful as the structurally different basket lane because it stress-tests the retest thesis on a different volatility personality while staying inside the allowed universe.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If AXS behaves as pure noise rather than structured retests, the continuation leg will not develop before the time-based de-risk triggers.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but the second leg arrives too slowly, extend the early de-risk window slightly before tightening retest rules.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest hold and DI confirmation support each other
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad retest-distance rules

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by cutting 50% size
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14 by cutting 50% size
